title: Processing of direct RNA sequencing data for pseU modification detection - Selective RNA modification in situ by circular gRNAs in designer organelles
author: Wierczeiko, Anna
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains the bioinformatics scripts used for the pseudouridine modification quantification based on direct RNA sequencing data of HEK293T+reporter transcriptomes in the following publication: Schartel, Jann, Wierczeiko, Butto, Helm, Gerber, Lemke - Selective RNA modification in situ by circular gRNAs in designer organelles 
  Content:  
  1. Processing of raw data:  In the processing_RNA004.sh bash script, direct RNA sequencing (DRS) data is basecalled, mapped onto the reporter sequences and filtered for positions of interest. 
  2. Accuracy and Pseudouridine modification quantification:  The jupyter notebook Direct_RNA_seq_pattern_processing.ipynb contains the bascalling accuracy calculation of all samples and the subsequent pseudouridine quantification on specific positions by measuring the U>C mismatch frequency generated by the basecalling error and the dorado-based pseudouridine calling freqeuncy for remaining reads. 
  3. Visualization  We plotted the 10mer content around the modified positions in R.
